chunks.en: I usually run the washing machine at night, about three times a week.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい夜に、週三回くらい洗濯機を回します。

chunks.en: The first thing I do is separate the white clothes from the dark ones.
chunks.ja: 最初にすることは、白い服を濃い色の服と分けることです。

chunks.en: I dry my clothes inside on rainy days, and they take about half a day.
chunks.ja: 雨の日は部屋干しをして、乾くまで半日くらいかかります。

chunks.en: Honestly, I often put off folding the laundry until the next morning.
chunks.ja: 正直に言うと、洗濯物をたたむのを翌朝まで後回しにしがちです。

outline: Opening: when and how often I do laundry
Point 1: sorting clothes and picking a setting
Point 2: drying at home and how long it takes
Wrap-up: folding is the part I always delay

modelTalk.text: Let me tell you how I do my laundry. I usually run the washing machine at night. It's quieter then, and I've got more free time. I do it about three times a week. Before I start, I sort my clothes. I put the white things in one pile and the dark ones in another. Then I pick a gentle setting and cool water. That way my clothes last longer. I don't have a dryer, so I hang everything up inside. It takes about a day to dry. In summer it's faster, maybe half a day. The hard part is folding. I always put it off. Sometimes the clean clothes just sit on my chair for days. But when I finally fold them and put them away, my room feels tidy again. Then I feel really good. That's my simple laundry routine.

chunks.en: I usually run the washing machine at night, about three times a week.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい夜に、週に3回くらい洗濯機を回します。

chunks.en: First, I sort my clothes and pick a gentle setting for dark colors.
chunks.ja: まず服を分けて、色物にはやさしいコースを選びます。

chunks.en: After that, I hang my clothes inside because I don't have a dryer.
chunks.ja: そのあとは乾燥機がないので、部屋の中に服を干します。

chunks.en: In the end, I often put off folding the clothes until the next day.
chunks.ja: 結局、服をたたむのを次の日まで後回しにしがちです。

outline: Opening: when and how often I do laundry
Point 1: sorting clothes and choosing a setting
Point 2: drying clothes at home without a dryer
Wrap-up: folding is the part I put off

modelTalk.text: I usually do my laundry at night. I run the washing machine about three times a week. In the morning I'm too busy, so night is easier for me. Before I start, I sort my clothes. I keep the white ones away from the dark ones. Then I pick a gentle setting and cold water. Cold water is fine for most of my clothes. I don't have a dryer, so I hang everything inside my room. It usually takes a day to dry. When the weather is nice, I hang things outside. That's much faster. After they're dry, I fold them and put them away. But honestly, I often leave the clean pile on my chair. I tell myself I'll do it later. Then it just sits there for days. I really should fix that habit soon.
